column4: NIST SRM 1720 archived Certificate of Analysis information-value table and mixture-preparation transcription
column5: The lot was whole ambient air filled at Niwot Ridge Colorado in 2010 and scrubbed of moisture to represent northern continental air. The information table reports oxygen 20.93 mole percent, argon 0.935 mole percent, carbon monoxide 130 to 180 nanomoles per mole, and nitrogen as balance. These are explicitly information values that cannot establish metrological traceability; the carbon-monoxide endpoints are an observed information range, not confidence bounds. The generic archived certificate leaves cylinder-specific certified carbon-dioxide methane and nitrous-oxide values and expanded uncertainties as SAMPLE placeholders. Cylinder and sample identity, residual humidity, use-state temperature and pressure, remaining trace composition, and covariance are absent. Certification expired 2020-09-01. This one archived lot is not a universal dry-air composition or a standard-atmosphere model
